mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration triggers recession fears that compress equity multiples, generating negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a sharp drop in consumer credit balances signals a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins, depressing equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit statistically significant negative skew when both proxies decline simultaneously, or cross-validation IC drops below -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, rising consumer credit balances indicate resilient household spending that offsets tightening headwinds, supporting positive equity returns over the next week.
regime_hint: high-interest rate environment with sticky inflation
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly higher when credit expands under high rates, or the regime-specific alpha decays to zero over a 12-month out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically driving positive index returns over the subsequent month without requiring specific thresholds.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no predictive power for 20-day forward returns, or the sign of the relationship flips consistently across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index exceeds 95.0, supply chain normalization reduces input cost inflation, allowing corporate margins to expand and lifting equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns do not outperform when INDPRO remains below 95.0, or the threshold signal yields a negative Rank IC over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with a tightening federal funds rate creates a double contraction in aggregate demand and liquidity, severely compressing equity multiples over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series move in the specified direction, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During housing market booms, rising building permits signal strong consumer confidence and construction activity that spills over into broader economic growth, driving positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns show no positive correlation with permit growth during housing booms, or the predictive relationship reverses sign in out-of-sample periods exceeding 12 months.
